core.action: Verify that each required config fields are provided at construction — field names automatically become strategy attributes; missing required fields or typos in field names will not raise validation errors unless on_config is overridden

consequence.description: Silent typos in config field names result in missing strategy attributes without errors, causing strategies to fail with AttributeError at runtime when accessing misspelled parameters

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-010

core.when: When implementing strategy on_bar or on_history methods that receive bar data
core.action: Assume strategies can receive incomplete or partial bars during bar aggregation — the framework finalizes bars internally via internal queue and only publishes completed OHLCV bars on bar close

consequence.description: Strategies written to handle streaming or partial bar updates will never receive such data in backtesting, creating look-ahead bias where live trading signals differ from backtested behavior when receiving only closed bars

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-002

core.when: When implementing Actor instance initialization in multi-strategy systems
core.action: Initialize each Actor instances (Strategies, ExecutionEngine, RiskEngine, etc.) sequentially under Trader's control, ensuring each dependency is fully initialized before the next begins — never use parallel or concurrent initialization

consequence.description: Concurrent initialization of Actor instances causes race conditions where dependent components access uninitialized resources, leading to crashes, deadlocks, or silent state corruption that manifests only under specific timing conditions

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-022

core.when: When implementing position calculation logic in backtesting or live trading
core.action: Use fill-based aggregation model where each fill updates an internal cost-bar — calculate position state from completed fills deterministically, never from real-time price feeds

consequence.description: Using streaming mark-to-market breaks broker parity; position values differ from broker statements, causing reconciliation failures and live trading account mismatches that are difficult to debug

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-021

core.when: When implementing clock or time simulation in backtest engine
core.action: Verify data timestamps are monotonically non-decreasing — use a single static Clock advanced by BacktestEngine and process events strictly in timestamp order to guarantee deterministic execution and reproducibility

consequence.description: Non-monotonic timestamps cause events to be processed out of causal order, making backtest results irreproducible across runs and creating discrepancies between backtested and live trading behavior

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-026

core.when: When implementing strategy design for event sourcing or backtest replay
core.action: Verify that strategy can be fully reconstructed from the event stream using strategy_id and position_id attached to order events — verify strategies hold no mutable state required for reconstruction

consequence.description: Strategies relying on mutable state cannot be reconstructed from event logs alone, breaking audit trails, backtest replay, and compliance requirements that depend on stateless event sourcing

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-008

core.when: When implementing funding rate calculations for perpetual futures
core.action: Verify that funding rate is TWAPed over the funding interval (typically 8 hours) rather than applied instantaneously at funding timestamps — check that cost/proceeds are distributed smoothly across the interval

consequence.description: Instantaneous funding application creates artificial PnL discontinuities at funding timestamps, misrepresenting actual trader PnL and causing backtest results to diverge from live trading performance

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-033

core.when: When implementing factor computation that involves matrix operations (e.g., factor regression, covariance matrix calculation, portfolio optimization)
core.action: Assume the framework handles matrix ill-conditioning — matrix inversion, eigenvalue decomposition, or linear system solving without regularization or condition number checks will produce unreliable results

consequence.description: Factor computations with ill-conditioned matrices produce numerically unstable results, causing strategies to receive incorrect factor values and generate erroneous trading signals that deviate significantly from expected performance

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-001

core.when: When implementing factor computation with matrix operations
core.action: Add regularization (Tikhonov/L2), check condition number before solving linear systems, use SVD-based pseudoinverse for matrices with condition_number > 10000, and implement fallback to numerically stable algorithms

consequence.description: Without regularization, ill-conditioned matrices in factor computations cause numerical overflow or underflow, leading to corrupted factor values that produce trading signals opposite to intended strategy behavior

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-001

core.when: When implementing order submission or modification logic
core.action: Verify every order passes through RiskEngine._check_order before reaching ExecutionEngine — orders must never bypass the risk gate regardless of component refactoring or optimization attempts

consequence.description: Bypassing risk checks allows unconstrained orders to reach the venue, causing potential runaway positions, margin calls exceeding account equity, or regulatory violations that result in significant financial losses

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-012

core.when: When implementing order state querying or caching logic
core.action: Query order states from ExecutionEngine via MessageBus events — do not cache or hold local copies of order state in Portfolio, Cache, or Strategy components

consequence.description: Maintaining separate order state copies causes split-brain scenarios where components disagree on order status, leading to incorrect position calculations, duplicate orders, or missed fills that corrupt backtest and live trading results

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-015

core.when: When configuring order management for single-instrument strategies
core.action: Use OmsType.NETTING to automatically net opposing fills into a single position state — do not use HEDGING mode for single instruments as it adds unnecessary venue complexity

consequence.description: Using HEDGING mode for single-instrument strategies creates unnecessary opposing positions, increasing transaction costs and causing incorrect P&L attribution that misrepresents actual strategy performance

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-038

core.when: When setting up order management for single-instrument strategies
core.action: Verify OmsType configuration matches strategy requirements before live trading — NETTING for single-instrument, HEDGING for multi-instrument — and confirm the default matches expected netting behavior

consequence.description: Default OmsType configuration may not match strategy requirements, causing position tracking errors when mismatched configuration leads to unexpected netting or lack of netting in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-038

core.when: When configuring position sizing for a strategy
core.action: Set leverage to match the target instrument's margin requirements — Decimal(1) = 1:1 no leverage, Decimal(10) = 10x leverage — verify the leverage setting aligns with account risk parameters before enabling margin

consequence.description: Mismatched leverage causes incorrect position sizing relative to account equity, either underutilizing capital or exceeding margin requirements and triggering forced liquidations during adverse market moves

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-040

core.when: When using the framework's default leverage parameter
core.action: Verify default_leverage=Decimal(1) matches the target instrument's actual margin requirements — for futures and margin-enabled instruments, adjust to required leverage ratio before live trading

consequence.description: Default leverage of 1x may not match futures or margin-enabled instruments requiring specific leverage ratios, causing insufficient position sizing or margin call mismatches that result in losses

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-040

core.when: When configuring execution engine settings for production backtesting
core.action: Verify snapshot_positions=True remains enabled to persist position states to database for audit trail and system failure recovery

consequence.description: Disabling position snapshots breaks the audit trail required for compliance, and eliminates the ability to reconstruct trading state after system failures, potentially causing regulatory violations and operational data loss

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-062

core.when: When implementing state management for Actors/Strategies in crash-only recovery scenarios
core.action: Verify that on_save is explicitly called to persist state before any potential crash or failure, as the crash-only design pattern relies on state being available in the persisted cache for reload on startup

consequence.description: Without explicit state persistence via on_save, crash-only recovery will reload stale or empty state, causing strategies to lose positions, capital tracking, and other critical runtime state

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-011

core.when: When submitting orders via OrderFactory outside an active trading session
core.action: Provide an explicit submit_order_handler when creating OrderFactory to verify orders are not silently discarded; verify OrderManager context exists before order submission

consequence.description: Submitting orders without proper OrderManager context causes silent order leakage where orders do nothing - no error is raised but orders are not executed, leading to missed trades that are difficult to diagnose

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-109

core.when: When implementing or modifying Position.reopen logic to restore trading positions
core.action: Verify Position.reopen clears each position state (unrealized PnL, quantity history, fill count, realized PnL) before applying the new fill - use fresh position semantics without any carryover from the closed position

consequence.description: Preserving any state during reopen (such as partial PnL or fill history) breaks position lifecycle semantics and creates inconsistent position states that do not reflect actual trading conditions, causing incorrect strategy decisions

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-114

core.when: When implementing portfolio-level risk management with profit_threshold and loss_limit parameters
core.action: Verify that threshold checks use portfolio snapshot state rather than streaming updates to verify consistent portfolio state across each position evaluations

consequence.description: Using streaming PnL updates instead of snapshot state can cause false triggers where threshold checks see inconsistent portfolio views, leading to premature or delayed position closures that distort backtest risk metrics

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-014

core.when: When implementing or refactoring OrderEmulator and related execution logic
core.action: Verify OrderEmulator maintains its internal simulation path through DataEngine subscription and ExecutionEngine, preserving the uniform venue interface where emulated fills are indistinguishable from real fills

consequence.description: Refactoring OrderEmulator to use separate paths breaks the uniform venue interface, causing risk engine and strategies to misidentify fill sources and produce incorrect risk calculations

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-016

core.when: When setting up backtesting with FillModel and conditional orders
core.action: Verify that prob_fill_on_limit=0.2 matches actual market liquidity conditions or calibrate against historical fill rate data for the target market

consequence.description: Using default prob_fill_on_limit=0.2 without calibration causes backtests to assume unrealistic fill probabilities, leading to strategies that overpromise execution quality in live trading with actual fill rates that may be significantly lower

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-042

core.when: When calculating free balance for margin accounts
core.action: Use min(availableBalance, walletBalance) as the free balance calculation — availableBalance alone must NOT be used as it may include collateral tied to open positions

consequence.description: Using availableBalance directly for free balance creates hidden leverage risk when open positions consume collateral; in live trading, over-trading beyond actual available funds triggers margin calls or forced liquidation

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-094

core.when: When implementing market maker quote distribution logic
core.action: Use inventory-based dynamic grid spacing that adjusts quote distribution based on current position exposure — do NOT use fixed uniform grid spacing

consequence.description: Fixed grid spacing without inventory adjustment causes accumulation of one-sided inventory, leading to locked positions that must be closed at unfavorable market prices with high slippage

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-092

core.when: When implementing position sizing logic for risk management
core.action: Calculate unit batch sizes aligned with instrument lot sizes using discrete unit calculation — do NOT use continuous floating-point sizing that ignores exchange minimum order size constraints

consequence.description: Continuous sizing without unit batch alignment produces order sizes below exchange minimums, causing order rejections and missed entries on high-conviction trading signals

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-097

core.when: When routing orders on Polymarket binary markets
core.action: Determine order side based on trader role: makers take the YES side, takers take the NO side — do NOT use generic order side assignment that ignores Polymarket's bilateral market structure

consequence.description: Generic order side assignment without understanding Polymarket's maker=taker YES/NO convention routes orders to the wrong outcome leg, causing order rejections or fills on unintended positions

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-099

core.when: When configuring ExecEngineConfig for high-frequency strategy execution
core.action: Maintain allow_overfills=True for HFT strategies to prevent unnecessary order rejections due to strict lot size constraints; if changing to False, verify position accounting accurately reflects actual fills and lot enforcement

consequence.description: Setting allow_overfills=False may cause order rejections in high-frequency strategies due to lot size mismatches during rapid position accumulation, leading to missed executions and execution delays that materially impact HFT returns

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-089

core.when: When configuring StrategyConfig for strategies using contingent orders (OTO/OCO)
core.action: Enable manage_contingent_orders=True to verify linked orders are properly activated and canceled according to strategy rules; if disabling, implement manual contingent order management to prevent orphaned orders

consequence.description: Disabling contingent order management causes orphaned OTO/OCO orders that never activate or cancel properly, resulting in strategies missing exit signals and accumulating unintended positions with no automatic resolution

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-091

core.when: When implementing position sizing, PnL calculation, or any monetary arithmetic
core.action: Use Decimal type for each monetary quantities (prices, quantities, PnL, costs) instead of float or double to eliminate binary floating-point rounding errors in financial calculations

consequence.description: Float arithmetic in financial calculations introduces rounding errors that compound over many trades, causing PnL reports to diverge from actual returns and potentially triggering incorrect position sizing or risk management decisions

evidence_refs.summary: 3869566 Derived from BD: BD-093

core.when: When adding new instrument types to the trading system
core.action: Explicitly decide whether new instrument types belong in ENGINE_EXPIRING_INSTRUMENT_CLASSES whitelist for automatic expiration handling or require manual expiration processing; do not assume automatic inclusion or exclusion

consequence.description: Adding instruments without determining expiration handling causes incorrect behavior: non-expiring instruments processed through expiration logic or expiring instruments missing expiration, leading to stale positions and incorrect backtest results
